title: Progressive leg weakness in a 34-year-old post-diarrheal illness
presentation: A 34-year-old male presents with 1 week of progressive bilateral leg weakness now spreading to his arms and difficulty swallowing. He had Campylobacter gastroenteritis 3 weeks ago. Vitals: BP 132/84, HR 92, RR 20, SpO2 96%. Exam: symmetric ascending flaccid weakness worse in legs, absent deep tendon reflexes throughout, bilateral facial weakness, no sensory level, intact cognition.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Ascending flaccid paralysis + absent reflexes + post-infectious = GBS. Albuminocytologic dissociation on LP (high protein, normal cells) confirms. Monitor FVC — intubate if <20ml/kg or NIF <25cmH2O. IVIG or plasmapheresis. Autonomic instability is deadly.
